acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `bd show --help` no longer shows `--namespace` by default.
- [ ] Protocol-only flags are hidden from default help across ordinary command pages unless an explicit advanced-help path is requested.
- [ ] The hidden flags remain supported and discoverable via an explicit advanced/help-doc path.
- [ ] Tests cover both parsing and help visibility policy.
description:
**Problem**
Advanced protocol and consistency flags currently clutter nearly every help page in beads-rs: `--namespace`, `--durability`, `--client-request-id`, `--require-min-seen`, and `--wait-timeout-ms`. These are real capabilities, but they are not normal day-to-day CLI UX, and `--namespace` in particular should not be visible by default.

**Files:** crates/beads-cli/src/cli.rs, any help/render plumbing added for clap output policy
design: Define an explicit policy for advanced transport/protocol flags: they must remain parseable and documented somewhere, but should not appear in default help for common commands. Treat `bd show --help` as the concrete pain-point proof, then apply the same policy consistently across ordinary command help surfaces.
title: Hide advanced protocol flags from default CLI help
